source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane Other Lifting

event.incident_type: Slip/Trip/Fall - Hand Injury from Pinch Point
actions_taken: Injured person reported to rig medic for medical treatment
Injured person evacuated to shore for further evaluation and medical treatment
BSEE incident investigator traveled to rig on April 11, 2018
Onsite investigation conducted with rig floor photos and witness interviews
event.summary: On April 5, 2018, at approximately 14:50 hours, a floor hand on the Ensco 75 drilling rig sustained a crush injury to his left ring finger when it became caught between a five-inch drill pipe stand and a pup joint during a spacing out operation. The injured person was using a short piece of manila rope to stabilize the drill pipe instead of available hands-free tools such as push poles or a drill pipe connection guide. The final diagnosis was a fracture to the tip of the left ring finger. The incident resulted in evacuation to shore and loss of time accident (>3 days).
recommendations: Issue a safety alert to address the importance of utilizing available Hands Free Tools when working with drill pipe to avoid potential hand injuries

hazards.name: Pinch Point Between Drill Pipe and Pup Joint

threats.name: Improper Hand Placement During Drill Pipe Handling
Inadequate Stabilization of Drill Pipe with Improper Equipment

controls.name: Hands-Free Tools (Push Poles and Drill Pipe Connection Guide)
controls.barrier_role: Prevent hand placement in pinch points during drill pipe handling
controls.lod_basis: Primary engineering control to eliminate hand contact with drill pipe

supporting_text: Push poles and a drill pipe connection guide was available on the rig floor at the time of the incident and would have prevented rig crew personnel from placing hands on the DP.
Although, there were other Hands Free tools available on the rig floor.

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.barrier_role: Ensure proper procedures and tool use are documented and communicated
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to establish and communicate safe work procedures

supporting_text: Lessee failed to ensure rig floor personnel performing the job task utilized Hands Free tools, which were available on the rig floor and outlined in the Job Safety Analysis.

controls.name: Stop Work Authority
controls.barrier_role: Empower personnel to halt unsafe work practices
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to enable intervention when unsafe practices are observed

supporting_text: Rig crew personnel did not utilize Stop Work Authority, while the IP was using the short piece of rope to stabilize and guide the DP stands.

controls.name: Proper Hand Placement Procedures
controls.barrier_role: Prevent hands from entering pinch point areas during drill pipe operations
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control establishing safe hand placement practices

supporting_text: Rig personnel failed to use proper hand placement when conducting operations, which lead to an occurrence of an injury from a pinch point hazard.
The IP placed his hand between the DP stand and the pup joint which is a potential pinch point.

controls.name: Hands-Free Tools Policy
controls.barrier_role: Establish and enforce organizational policy requiring hands-free tool use
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control establishing organizational safety standards

supporting_text: The Incident Investigator reviewed Ensco's Hands Free Tools policy with the Offshore Installation Manager (OIM).

controls.name: Medical Response and Evacuation
controls.barrier_role: Provide immediate medical treatment and evacuation for injured personnel
controls.lod_basis: Recovery control to minimize consequences of injury

supporting_text: The IP reported to the rig Medic for medical treatment. A shore based Physician was consulted and the IP was evacuated to shore for further evaluation and medical treatment.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
